mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real retail sales reflects robust consumer demand that drives corporate revenue expectations higher, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: RSAFS three-month growth positively predicts S&P 500 twenty-day direction in less than fifty-five percent of out-of-sample months.

mechanism_narrative: A three-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals accelerating labor market deterioration that triggers broad risk-off repositioning, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next sixty trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts positive sixty-day returns in more than fifty percent of instances when UNRATE has risen greater than 0.5pp over three months.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate sits below one percent while consumer price inflation accelerates above two percent year-over-year, negative real interest rates reduce the opportunity cost of equities and inflate nominal corporate revenues, jointly driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: Joint signal of low FEDFUNDS and high CPIAUCSL fails to generate positive twenty-day returns in greater than fifty-five percent of triggered periods over a rolling three-year window.

mechanism_narrative: During periods immediately following large industrial contractions, month-over-month industrial production growth exceeding one percent signals a rebound in supply-side output that restores corporate earnings estimates, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration greater than one percent month-over-month does not produce positive five-day S&P 500 returns in at least sixty percent of observations during post-shock recovery periods.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above five percent indicates restrictive monetary policy that raises discount rates and debt service costs, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive twenty-day returns in more than fifty-five percent of months when FEDFUNDS exceeds five percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate indicate a tightening labor market that boosts aggregate wage income and consumption, jointly predicting positive S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases month-over-month and UNRATE decreases month-over-month
failure_condition: Joint PAYEMS increase and UNRATE decrease periods are followed by negative S&P 500 twenty-day returns more than forty percent of the time over a three-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In the aftermath of recessionary troughs, month-over-month housing starts growth exceeding five percent signals a durable revival in interest-sensitive demand that lifts construction and consumer durable sectors, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next sixty trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST accelerations are not followed by positive S&P 500 sixty-day returns in at least fifty-five percent of instances during post-recession recoveries.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month contraction in industrial production signals supply-side weakness that reduces manufacturing profitability and drags broad corporate earnings expectations lower, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO month-over-month declines fail to predict negative five-day S&P 500 returns with a hit rate above fifty-five percent over any two-year rolling window.
